name: QGIS
description: Full-featured open-source desktop GIS. Create, edit, visualize, and analyze geospatial data with extensive plugin ecosystem.

name: GRASS GIS
description: Geospatial processing engine for raster, vector, and temporal data. 400+ modules for terrain modeling, hydrology, and imagery.

name: Google Earth Pro
description: 3D earth visualization with historical imagery, GIS data import/export, and measurement tools. Free for desktop use.

name: GDAL/OGR
description: Translator library for 200+ raster and vector geospatial data formats. The backbone of most open-source GIS software.

name: PROJ
description: Coordinate transformation library for cartographic projections and geodetic computations. Core dependency for most GIS tools.

name: Shapely
description: Python library for manipulation and analysis of planar geometric objects. Wraps the GEOS library with a Pythonic API.

name: GeoPandas
description: Extends Pandas with geospatial operations. GeoDataFrames for spatial joins, overlays, and plotting with Shapely geometries.

name: Turf.js
description: Modular geospatial analysis engine for JavaScript and TypeScript. Runs in the browser or Node.js with GeoJSON in/out.

name: PostGIS
description: Spatial database extension for PostgreSQL. Adds geometry types, spatial indexing, and hundreds of geospatial functions.

name: H3
description: Hexagonal hierarchical geospatial indexing system by Uber. Partition the globe into hexagons at 16 resolutions for spatial analysis.

name: Leaflet
description: Lightweight JavaScript library for mobile-friendly interactive maps. 42 KB, extensible with hundreds of plugins.

name: Mapbox GL JS
description: JavaScript library for interactive, customizable vector maps. WebGL-powered with 60 FPS rendering on desktop and mobile.

name: OpenLayers
description: High-performance web mapping library. Renders tiles, vector data, and markers from GeoJSON, KML, GML, and other formats.

name: CesiumJS
description: Open-source JavaScript library for 3D globes and maps. WebGL-accelerated with terrain, imagery, and 3D Tiles support.

name: deck.gl
description: WebGL-powered framework for large-scale data visualization on maps. Renders millions of data points with GPU acceleration.

name: Kepler.gl
description: Browser-based geospatial analysis tool for large datasets by Uber. Drop in CSV or GeoJSON for instant map visualizations.
